title: Churchwardens’ Accounts and Memoranda of St Mary-at-Hill, 1420–1559 (Littlehales Chapters V–VI selection)
summary: The pinned repository XML copies U-M CME item ajt8135, which encodes a bounded V–VI selection from two distinct Littlehales EETS carriers: 1904 title leaves [iii]–[iv] and pp. 61–356, and 1905 title leaves [iii]–[iv] and pp. 357–413. Historical MS A supplies the continuous text; MS B contributes only the memorandum at p. 240, leaf 62. Historical MS A remains separate from the three current TLA /001 components because no institutional split or folio concordance was found. Eleven fixity-verified cache objects represent the two Internet Archive carriers and the official TLA catalog PDF.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for ajt8135
entities.description: The exact pinned XML artifact consumed by this repository. It preserves the bounded U-M encoding and remains distinct from the U-M digital entity, the two printed editions, their scans, and all manuscript witnesses.
notes: The file is 884,307 bytes. The 82 DIV2 and 563 DIV3 containers are encoding metrics, not intellectual-item counts.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME encoding ajt8135
entities.description: U-M DLPS electronic text created in 2003 from Littlehales’s 1904–1905 edition. The item merges bounded portions of two distinct printed carriers into one digital text.

date_statements.label: U-M publication/encoding date
date_statements.notes: The header says it was created by hand from the text and MARC record on 2003-03-26.

notes: The collection-level production history does not identify whether this exact item was obtained from OTA, manually keyed by U-M, or contributed.
The current item’s No Copyright-US statement and the retained 2003 redistribution restriction are preserved as separate rights layers.

entities.label: Littlehales, Medieval Records of a London City Church, Part I (1904)
entities.description: The distinct 1904 EETS OS 125 carrier. The encoded selection uses title leaves [iii]–[iv] and printed pp. 61–356; the carrier itself is not merged with Part II.

entities.label: Littlehales, Medieval Records of a London City Church, Part II (1905)
entities.description: The distinct 1905 EETS OS 128 carrier. The encoded selection uses title leaves [iii]–[iv] and printed pp. 357–413; printed pp. 414–449 and the remaining carrier are outside the target.

bibliographic.citation: Henry Littlehales, ed., The Medieval Records of a London City Church (St. Mary at Hill) A.D. 1420–1559, Part II, EETS Original Series 128 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1905).

entities.label: Littlehales’s historical MS A: Guildhall Library MS 1239/1
entities.description: The one-volume, 821-numbered-leaf state described and used by Littlehales. It supplies the continuous target text. Its exact transformation into the three current TLA /001 components and their current foliation remains unresolved.

holding.notes: The underlying parish records are now represented by TLA catalog components, but no direct identity is asserted between this historical codicological state and any one current /001 component.

physical_description.support: Paper leaves in a stout parchment binding
physical_description.extent: 821 numbered leaves; an unknown number of opening leaves already missing
physical_description.dimensions: approximately 12.5 × 10 × 6 inches as described by Littlehales
notes: Probably bound about 1559.
Littlehales reports proper order through 1537 except for the Porth inventory and later disturbance of leaves 696–763; he prints the later records in chronological editorial order.
No same_as, contains, or direct transcribes relation is asserted between this entity and current /001/001–003.
Current component date ranges do not substitute for a folio concordance.

entities.label: TLA P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/001/001
entities.description: Current TLA Document-level component record. It is not equated to all or part of historical MS A without an institutional concordance.
notes: The generic current scope note is repeated across all four records and is not a folio-level concordance to Littlehales.

entities.label: TLA P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/001/002
entities.description: Current TLA Document-level component record. It is not equated to all or part of historical MS A without an institutional concordance.
notes: Rayment’s use of historical MS 1239/1 part 2 folios supports later part terminology but not a complete institutional transformation or folio crosswalk.

entities.label: TLA P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/001/003
entities.description: Current TLA Document-level component record. Harley explicitly calls it former Guildhall Library MS 1239/1 part 3, but no complete concordance identifies its relation to the historical 821-leaf MS A state.

historical_designations: Guildhall Library MS 1239/1, part 3 in later scholarship

notes: Scholarship cites part 3 folios whose dates conflict with a simple date-range split; the conflict remains open rather than being normalized.

entities.label: Littlehales MS B / TLA P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/002
entities.description: One supported-identity witness joining Littlehales’s historical MS B designation to current TLA /002. Within the cataloged selection it contributes only the 1500–01 memorandum at printed p. 240 from leaf 62.

date_statements.label: Historical contents and binding described by Littlehales

physical_description.extent: About half blank; first 114 leaves and two leaves near the end used, as described by Littlehales
physical_description.dimensions: approximately 20 × 12 × 3 inches as described by Littlehales
notes: Littlehales says the binding appears to be by the same binder as MS A and dates about 1559.
The volume contains abbreviated duplicates and other materials including wills and inventories.
The supported identity is not an asserted documented migration event.
MS B is not a general second witness for Chapter VI.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan carrier 30081003328311 (Part I)
entities.description: Complete 424-page scan carrier and derivative family for the 1904 Part I volume. The target occupies title leaves [iii]–[iv] and printed pp. 61–356.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, page-number map, scandata, and item metadata

entities.label: Internet Archive scan carrier medievalrecordso0000henr (Part II)
entities.description: Complete currently retrievable 202-page PDF carrier and derivative family for the 1905 Part II volume. The target occupies title leaves [iii]–[iv] and printed pp. 357–413.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, page-number map, scandata, and item metadata
notes: The metadata says collection_set=printdisabled, but the exact PDF and derivatives recorded here were directly publicly retrievable on 2026-07-16 and had no per-file private flag in the live metadata API.

entities.label: The London Archives P69/MRY4 catalog-description layer
entities.description: The official current collection-list PDF and public catalog-description layer for St Mary-at-Hill parish records. It describes four current MS01239 component records but contains no target manuscript text or page images.
entities.reproduction_format: Public catalog records and official collection-list PDF

rights.component: Represented target text from the 1904–1905 Littlehales edition
rights.basis: The repository artifact represents target passages from the London-published 1904–1905 editions. Under 17 U.S.C. § 104A(a)(1)(B), any restored copyright could subsist only for the remainder of the U.S. term the work would otherwise have received; § 304 caps the relevant pre-1978 term at 95 years from securing. Even assuming maximum U.S. protection, the later 1905 edition expired no later than 2000. This U.S.-only row covers represented edition text, not the XML serialization or markup, and makes no claim about source-country status.

rights.component: Repository XML serialization, data structure, and markup layer
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: CME’s root LICENSE contains the Unlicense text but explicitly calls its subject software. No evidence establishes that it grants rights in inherited XML, data, or markup, so no license is assigned to this layer.

rights.component: Current U-M item-page digital text and presentation layer
rights.basis: The exact current U-M item says the materials are public domain and supplies the No Copyright–United States rights statement.
notes: No Copyright-US is jurisdiction-specific and does not silently license third-party interfaces, scan layers, or the downstream repository artifact worldwide.

rights.component: Retained 2003 XML availability and redistribution-terms layer
rights.basis: The retained 2003 availability paragraph permits individual copying for personal use, research, and teaching and permits linking, but expressly forbids redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers. Its current supersession and component scope are unresolved.
notes: This dated restriction is preserved separately from the current No Copyright-US statement; neither silently erases the other.

rights.component: Part I edition text and historical editorial apparatus
rights.basis: The title page records London publication in 1904. Under 17 U.S.C. § 104A(a)(1)(B), any restored copyright could subsist only for the remainder of the U.S. term the edition would otherwise have received; § 304 caps the relevant pre-1978 term at 95 years from securing. Even assuming maximum U.S. protection, the term expired no later than 1999. This conclusion is limited to the United States and does not assert United Kingdom or other source-country status.
notes: The same-entity edition access reaches the text through the public IA carrier; the scan and delivery layer remains covered by its separate facsimile rights row.

rights.component: Part II edition text and historical editorial apparatus
rights.basis: The title page records London publication in 1905. Under 17 U.S.C. § 104A(a)(1)(B), any restored copyright could subsist only for the remainder of the U.S. term the edition would otherwise have received; § 304 caps the relevant pre-1978 term at 95 years from securing. Even assuming maximum U.S. protection, the term expired no later than 2000. This conclusion is limited to the United States and does not assert United Kingdom or other source-country status.
notes: The same-entity edition access reaches the text through the public IA carrier; the scan and delivery layer remains covered by its separate facsimile rights row.

rights.component: Only the target MS A passages as published in the 1904–1905 Littlehales edition
rights.basis: This row covers only historical MS A passages as represented in the London-published 1904–1905 editions. Under 17 U.S.C. § 104A(a)(1)(B), any restored copyright could subsist only for the remainder of the U.S. term the editions would otherwise have received; § 304 caps the relevant pre-1978 term at 95 years from securing. Even assuming maximum U.S. protection, the later 1905 term expired no later than 2000. This U.S.-only conclusion does not clear unpublished matter elsewhere in the witness, any new manuscript image, or source-country rights.
notes: The enquiry access preserves a real route without implying a resolved modern component identity or direct physical access.

rights.component: Underlying manuscript text of the current /001/001 physical component
rights.basis: The London-published 1904 representation is public domain in the United States because any restored copyright under 17 U.S.C. § 104A(a)(1)(B) could last only for the U.S. term capped by § 304 at 95 years from securing, which expired no later than 1999. But the historical-MS-A/current-component folio concordance is unresolved, so no exact /001/001 leaves or passages can inherit that conclusion. Rights in the underlying manuscript text of this current physical component are therefore unknown; no source-country status is asserted.

rights.component: Any TLA-created or TLA-supplied manuscript image or reproduction
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: TLA says copying depends on condition and copyright, private-study copies are for research, publication may require a licence, and third-party rights may exist because TLA does not own every collection.

rights.component: Underlying manuscript text of the current /001/002 physical component

But the historical-MS-A/current-component folio concordance is unresolved, so no exact /001/002 leaves or passages can inherit that conclusion. Rights in the underlying manuscript text of this current physical component are therefore unknown; no source-country status is asserted.

rights.component: Underlying manuscript text of the current /001/003 physical component

Although later scholarship calls this former MS 1239/1 part 3, the full folio concordance is unresolved, so no exact /001/003 leaves or passages can inherit that conclusion. rights.component: The p. 240 target memorandum from historical MS B leaf 62 as published in 1904
rights.basis: The exact target memorandum from historical MS B leaf 62 was published in the London 1904 Part I edition, and the identity and passage are supported. Under 17 U.S.C. § 104A(a)(1)(B), any restored copyright could subsist only for the remainder of the U.S. term the edition would otherwise have received; § 304 caps the relevant pre-1978 term at 95 years from securing. Even assuming maximum U.S. protection, the term expired no later than 1999. This U.S.-only row does not clear other unpublished matter, a new manuscript image, or source-country rights.

editorial_practices.source_feature: MS A accounts and memoranda, with one expressly identified MS B memorandum
editorial_practices.representation: MS A in full through 1494–95 and selected later matter through printed p. 356; the MS B memorandum is isolated at p. 240
editorial_practices.description: Littlehales prints MS A in full through 1494–95 and thereafter selects distinct-value matter while preserving the annual frame. Omitted matter is generally marked by asterisks or bracketed statements, although page headings and sums may be omitted silently.
editorial_practices.scope: Target printed pp. 61–356; the MS B contribution is only p. 240, leaf 62

editorial_practices.source_feature: Later MS A records, including a disturbed leaf sequence
editorial_practices.representation: Selected distinct-value matter presented in chronological editorial order
editorial_practices.description: For later MS A matter Littlehales retains the annual account frame, selects material judged to have distinct value, and reorders disturbed leaves chronologically rather than asserting that the printed order is the manuscript’s physical sequence.

editorial_practices.representation: Two title-page units and Chapters V–VI only
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding joins title leaves and bounded chapter ranges from the two carriers. The inherited source note calling them Chapters IV and V is contradicted by the encoded body, contents, and continuous pp. 61–413.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned source XML, ajt8135.xml
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT; BODY; XML node ajt8135:4.6.3
evidence.summary: The 884,307-byte XML supplies the U-M 2003 header, exact source-edition statement, title pages, Chapters V–VI, printed-page continuity, historical MS 1239/1 and 1239/2 source note, and the one explicit MS B memorandum at p. 240/leaf 62.

evidence.citation: Independent structural, page-boundary, omission, gap, language, and MS B occurrence audit of ajt8135.xml
evidence.locator: FRONT/BODY; DIV1–DIV3; HEAD; P; TABLE/ROW/CELL; PB/EPB; MILESTONE; GAP; NOTE1; L; language declarations and body text
evidence.summary: Manual and structural inspection found 4 DIV1, 82 DIV2, 563 DIV3, 722 HEAD, 724 P, 578 TABLE, 5,861 ROW, 11,883 CELL, 8 L, 0 LG, 357 PB, 358 EPB, 1,359 MILESTONE, 172 SUPPLIED, 41 GAP, 38 NOTE1, and 3 FIGURE elements. Thirty-three subordinate DIV3 nodes are explicit omission placeholders. The body has consecutive PB 61–413, one MS B contribution, no Greek characters, and no body language attributes.
notes: This evidence combines direct visual inspection with reproducible structural computation; serialized element counts are not intellectual-item counts.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME pinned Git object and introduction history for ajt8135.xml
evidence.summary: The file is Git blob 49a0fbb9c212f75ad5a4dcfff9c18a3128e4f090 at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c, the 2024-11-24 add-source-texts commit. No intervening repository-side transcription is asserted.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at pinned commit
evidence.summary: The 1,211-byte file contains the Unlicense text and explicitly calls its subject software. It does not establish whether inherited XML, data, or markup are included.
notes: Git blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Medieval records of a London city church, ajt8135
evidence.locator: item information, contents, access, and rights
evidence.summary: The exact item supplies the inherited title, a table of contents identifying Chapters V and VI, the 1904–1905 source context, public access, and the current public-domain/No Copyright–United States statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, About
evidence.summary: The collection history says CME began with texts obtained from OTA, transcribed by the Library, or contributed by faculty, and describes later manually keyed public-domain batches, proofreading, basic TEI, and bulk XML. It does not assign ajt8135 to one production batch.

evidence.citation: Cached exact Internet Archive Part I metadata object
evidence.summary: The 2,204-byte metadata XML identifies item 30081003328311, the 1904 date, St Mary-at-Hill, Littlehales, EETS OS 125/128, 424 images, and the public item route. It contains no rights or license field.

evidence.citation: Henry Littlehales, Medieval Records of a London City Church, Part I (1904), cached Internet Archive PDF
evidence.locator: title leaves [iii]–[iv]; printed pp. 61–356; p. 240; PDF pp. 107–412 for printed pp. 61–356
evidence.summary: The 16,037,760-byte, 424-page PDF verifies the distinct 1904 Part I carrier, target title leaves and pp. 61–356, Chapter V and the first portion of Chapter VI, and the p. 240 note identifying the memorandum as from MS B leaf 62 with a less explicit MS A reference.

evidence.citation: Cached Internet Archive OCR text for Littlehales Part I
evidence.summary: The 699,042-byte OCR derivative supplies searchable text for the complete Part I carrier and supports reproducible checks against the title, target page range, and MS B memorandum. The PDF remains authoritative for visual boundaries.

evidence.citation: Cached Internet Archive Part I page-number map
evidence.summary: The 71,154-byte page-number map verifies that printed p. 61 maps to leafNum 106/PDF p. 107 and printed p. 356 maps to leafNum 411/PDF p. 412. It is metadata-only and contains no target text or image.

evidence.citation: Cached Internet Archive Part I scandata
evidence.summary: The 148,956-byte scandata XML records the complete carrier scan structure and supports the 424-page inventory. It is metadata-only and contains no target text or image.

evidence.citation: Cached exact Internet Archive Part II metadata object
evidence.summary: The 2,687-byte metadata XML identifies item medievalrecordso0000henr, Part II, 1905, 204 source images, and collection_set=printdisabled. The exact public derivatives used here had no per-file private flag when checked; the metadata contains no rights or license field.

evidence.citation: Henry Littlehales, Medieval Records of a London City Church, Part II (1905), cached Internet Archive PDF
evidence.locator: Part II introduction; title leaves [iii]–[iv]; printed pp. 357–413; PDF pp. 105–161
evidence.summary: The 10,023,449-byte, 202-page PDF verifies the distinct 1905 Part II carrier, target title leaves and pp. 357–413, and Littlehales’s descriptions of MS A and MS B, later MS A disturbance, selection, omission, and chronological-ordering methods. The full carrier continues through printed p. 449.

evidence.citation: Cached Internet Archive OCR text for Littlehales Part II
evidence.summary: The 407,044-byte OCR derivative supplies searchable text for the complete Part II carrier and supports checks of manuscript descriptions, target pages, and later records. The PDF remains authoritative for visual boundaries.

evidence.citation: Cached Internet Archive Part II page-number map
evidence.summary: The 34,227-byte page-number map verifies printed p. 413 at leafNum/PDF p. 161. It omits the printed p. 357 label, whose PDF p. 105 boundary was instead verified visually and textually. It contains no target text or image.

evidence.citation: Cached Internet Archive Part II scandata
evidence.summary: The 214,558-byte scandata XML records the complete carrier scan structure and supports the scan inventory. It is metadata-only and contains no target text or image.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, P69/MRY4 official collection list
evidence.summary: The 147,800-byte, 35-page official PDF lists the same four current Document records and date ranges as the live catalog. Its internal creation/modification date is 2024-08-05. It contains catalog metadata only, not target manuscript text or images.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/001/001
evidence.summary: The exact Document record is titled Churchwardens’ accounts, dated 1422–1505, at TLA, with former reference MS 01239 and the repeated generic Littlehales scope note. No item-linked digital object, closure, or restriction was displayed.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/001/002
evidence.summary: The exact Document record is titled Churchwardens’ accounts, dated 1506–1526, at TLA, with former reference MS 01239 and the repeated generic Littlehales scope note. No item-linked digital object, closure, or restriction was displayed.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/001/003
evidence.summary: The exact Document record is titled Churchwardens’ accounts, dated 1527–1559, at TLA, with former reference MS 01239 and the repeated generic Littlehales scope note. No item-linked digital object, closure, or restriction was displayed.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/002
evidence.summary: The exact Document record is titled Churchwardens’ accounts, dated 1485–1556, at TLA, with former reference MS 01239 and the repeated generic Littlehales scope note. The /002 sequence supports the approved historical MS 1239/2 identity; no item-linked digital object, closure, or restriction was displayed.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, About our catalogue
evidence.summary: TLA says its catalog is the gateway to its records, is incomplete and changing, and can be used after History Card registration to request records in advance. It supplies the official enquiry email ask@tla.libanswers.com.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, History Card
evidence.summary: The official page describes online pre-registration, required in-person completion, and the reader-registration route used to consult original records.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, Copying services for documents and images
evidence.summary: TLA offers remote assessment and copying subject to condition and copyright, with current assessment fees of £20 for one document and from £40 for multiple documents, additional to copying charges. It does not promise that a complete component can be copied.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, Copying permit
evidence.summary: The official page describes private-study copying conditions and states that self-captured images remain the copyright of the City of London. The provider assertion is preserved without adopting it as an independent legal conclusion about every underlying document.

evidence.citation: The London Archives, Licensing collections
evidence.summary: TLA explains that publication or reuse may require a licence, that it does not own every collection, and that third-party rights can apply. This evidence supports layered reproduction and catalog rights rather than a blanket conclusion.

evidence.citation: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, United States Code, title 17, §§ 104A(a)(1)(B) and 304(a)–(b)
evidence.locator: § 104A(a)(1)(B), restored copyright term; § 304(a)–(b), 28-year initial term plus 67-year renewal and 95-year total term
evidence.summary: Section 104A(a)(1)(B) limits any restored copyright to the remainder of the U.S. term the work would have received had it never entered the U.S. public domain. Section 304 gives the relevant pre-1978 copyrights a maximum 95-year term from the date copyright was secured. Accordingly, even assuming maximum U.S. protection, editions secured in 1904 and 1905 could not remain protected after 1999 and 2000, respectively; this evidence does not establish source-country status.

evidence.citation: Louise Rayment, ‘The Sixteenth-Century School at St. Mary-at-Hill, London,’ The London Journal 41.2 (2016), doi:10.1080/03058034.2016.1165787
evidence.summary: Rayment calls Guildhall Library MS 1239/1 a three-volume witness and cites part 2 at ff. 307v and 350r. This supports later part terminology but not an institutional split event or complete folio concordance.

evidence.citation: John Harley, Thomas Tallis (Ashgate, 2015; Routledge digital issue, 2016), publisher preview
evidence.summary: Harley explicitly identifies current LMA P69/MRY4/B/005/MS01239/001/003 as formerly Guildhall Library MS 1239/1, part 3, and cites original folios. The evidence supports that later component label but does not supply a complete three-component concordance.

evidence.citation: Bounded public-facsimile search for the four current TLA MS01239 components
evidence.summary: Exact current references, former shelfmarks, the official TLA collection PDF and item records, London Picture Archive search, and broader exact-shelfmark web searches revealed no public manuscript facsimile or item-linked page image for /001/001, /001/002, /001/003, or /002 on 2026-07-16.
notes: This date-bounded negative does not prove that TLA has no internal scans, preservation images, microfilm, or mediated reproduction.

open_questions.question: What exact institutional event and folio concordance relate Littlehales’s single 821-leaf Guildhall MS 1239/1 state to current /001/001, /001/002, and /001/003?
next_steps: Ask a TLA archivist for rebinding, conservation, accession, and old-to-new shelfmark documentation.
Request a complete old-folio-to-current-component concordance before adding same_as, contains, or direct transcribes relations.

open_questions.question: Why does the edition begin in 1420 while current /001/001 begins in 1422, and what does the repeated current catalog text ‘I, 1431–1553; w.&d.’ mean?
next_steps: Ask TLA to reconcile the generic scope note, component date ranges, opening losses, and retained original foliation.

open_questions.question: Can each of the four exact current Document records be ordered with a History Card today, and are any surfaces closed, at risk, unfit, surrogate-only, or otherwise restricted?
next_steps: Authenticate with a History Card or ask TLA staff to confirm production and condition status for each exact reference.
Record item-specific restrictions without generalizing one component’s status to the others.

open_questions.question: Do public, preservation, or service scans exist for each component, or will TLA assess complete all-surfaces reproduction requests with coverage, condition, price, format, and use terms?
next_steps: Submit a separate TLA copying assessment for each exact component.
Ask whether existing service or preservation images can be supplied before commissioning new capture.
Require explicit all-surfaces coverage and separate private-study and publication terms.

open_questions.question: Does the current public-domain/No Copyright-US item statement supersede the embedded 2003 redistribution restriction, and what exact digital components and jurisdictions does each statement cover?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for a component-level and jurisdiction-specific interpretation.
Retain both dated rights layers until U-M answers explicitly.

open_questions.question: Does CME’s root Unlicense, whose text calls its subject software, cover inherited XML, data, or markup?
next_steps: Obtain an explicit project statement before assigning the Unlicense to the XML/data/markup layer.
Keep represented public-domain edition text distinct from repository serialization rights.

open_questions.question: How should the current public Part II direct-file availability be reconciled with collection_set=printdisabled, and what rights, if any, govern the IA-created scan and delivery layer?
next_steps: Ask Internet Archive whether direct-file availability is intended and whether item-specific rights or access terms exist.
Do not infer a reuse license from public retrieval.

open_questions.question: Was ajt8135 manually keyed by U-M, obtained from the Oxford Text Archive, or contributed, and in which production batch?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections whether item-level production records survive.
Do not convert collection-level batch history into an item-level claim meanwhile.

review_notes: The graph has exactly 12 entities, 12 relations, 24 access records, 18 rights records, 30 evidence records, and 8 open questions.
Historical MS A remains separate from current /001/001–003; no same_as, contains, or direct transcribes edge is asserted without an institutional concordance.
MS B contributes exactly one scoped memorandum: XML ajt8135:4.6.3, printed p. 240, account year 1500–01, historical leaf 62.
The two Littlehales editions and two Internet Archive carrier entities remain distinct; no merged 1904–1905 carrier is invented.
All 11 local copies occur exactly once across three access records and total 27,788,881 bytes: four target-present complete PDF/OCR objects and seven target-absent metadata-only objects.

All 11 worktree cache files are byte-identical hardlinks to the canonical main cache, with matching inode, link count 2, and verified SHA-256; cached IA size and MD5 values also matched live file metadata on 2026-07-16.

Every modeled entity has at least one rights row and every rights row carries an access_id. Current U-M NoC-US, retained 2003 provider terms, edition public-domain status, repository markup uncertainty, IA scan uncertainty, and TLA reproduction terms remain separate layers.
No relation says the repository artifact consulted research sources merely because those sources were used to build this manifest.
